dataset: BBBC005 (Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection - Synthetic Cells)

pi_items.source: Task description and export_result.sh verification; verifier.py checks this path
Task description and export_result.sh verification; verifier.py checks this path

https://imagej.net/imaging/color-image-processing and https://imagej.net/plugins/replace-red-with-magenta confirm Magenta and Green are standard Fiji color names. Magenta-Green is a recognized colorblind accessibility technique.
https://colorblind.io/simulate/deuteranopia confirms Deuteranopia is red-green color blindness caused by missing green cones. Affects ~1% of males.

pi_items.verified_value: True - Magenta (Red+Blue) is distinguishable from Green even for deuteranopes who cannot perceive green light
pi_items.source: https://www.ascb.org/science-news/how-to-make-scientific-figures-accessible-to-readers-with-color-blindness/ and https://davidmathlogic.com/colorblind/ confirm Magenta/Green avoids red-green confusion. https://imagej.net/plugins/replace-red-with-magenta documents this accessibility technique in Fiji.

pi_items.verified_value: BBBC005 is a public dataset at https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005 containing 19,200 synthetic microscopy images (696x520 pixels, 8-bit TIF), generated via SIMCEP simulation platform
pi_items.source: https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005 confirms BBBC005 is public and contains simulated/synthetic cell images, not real cells. setup_task.sh attempts to download from https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/BBBC005_v1_images.zip

pi_items.metadata_value: Implicitly true (created from BBBC005 or generated)
pi_items.source: BBBC005 consists of synthetic/simulated images generated by SIMCEP platform (https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005). Fallback path (setup_task.sh lines 52-71) also generates synthetic data. Either way, data is synthetic.

pi_items.metadata_value: RGB color image (flattened, not a stack)
pi_items.verified_value: RGB color image (flattened, not a stack)
pi_items.source: Task description step 3: 'Convert the composite to a flattened RGB image'. verifier.py line 93 checks img_out.mode == 'RGB'.
Task description states this explicitly. setup_task.sh Open_Task_Image.ijm macro (lines 93-98) sets up Red for Stack.setChannel(1) and Green for Stack.setChannel(2).

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a real public dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing 19,200 synthetic microscopy images (696×520 pixels, 8-bit TIF) generated via SIMCEP simulation platform. The task converts a Red/Green composite microscopy image into a Magenta/Green composite, which is a recognized accessibility technique for Deuteranopia (absence of green-cone photoreceptors, affecting ~1% of males). Magenta (Red+Blue) is distinguishable from Green even for deuteranopes who cannot perceive red-green differences. The output is an RGB-flattened image where Output Red and Blue channels correlate with Input Channel 1 (proving Magenta composition), and Output Green correlates with Input Channel 2. All file paths, color names, and accessibility techniques are standard Fiji conventions documented in official ImageJ/Fiji documentation and colorblind accessibility research.
